message:
feat(actor,gen_server): differentiate stop (graceful drain) from abort (immediate exit)

Previously stop() and abort() on both ActorHandle and GenServerHandle
were effectively identical -- both set cancel_requested on the inner
context. This meant there was no way to gracefully stop an actor while
still honoring its mailbox drain contract; every stop was an abort.

Now the two have distinct semantics:

- stop() closes the mailbox sender and wakes the receiver. The actor
  loop detects the closed channel, enters the Stopping state, and
  drains all buffered messages before calling on_stop.
- abort() sets cancel_requested (as before) causing the loop to exit
  at the next cancellation checkpoint. During drain, aborted actors
  skip cast/info processing and just empty the mailbox.

The actor run loop now checks cx.is_cancel_requested() once at the
start of Phase 3 and branches:
- Graceful: process each remaining message via actor.handle().
- Aborted: discard messages via while let Ok(_msg) = try_recv().

GenServer drain similarly skips handle_cast/handle_info when aborted,
while still replying to pending calls with an error.

The on_stop callback now runs under cx.masked() so async cleanup
can proceed even if the context was previously aborted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
